## Target's compacted history
The target's earlier work has been folded into the `<summary>` block(s) below — this is what the target itself sees at the head of its context, NOT verbatim transcript. Use it to understand the arc: what was decided, what constraints were set, what was already done. If the window below shows the target acting on an assumption that CONTRADICTS a summary, re-doing work a summary says was completed, or ignoring a constraint a summary records — that is context drift; flag it. Do NOT trace values inside the summaries (they're compacted, not tool-verifiable). Do NOT emit `msg_idx` pointing into this section — `msg_idx` is window-relative. If a summary's paraphrase is ambiguous on the point you're checking, `query_target_history(summary=<id>, question=…)` fetches the original behind it — use sparingly.

[2 earlier summary block(s) elided — over the 30000-char prefix cap]

<summary id=61x2fwjt>
CHIME per-burst ACF de-scalloping fix + RFI investigation + interactive window editor build (msgs e1–e133)

## De-scalloping the per-burst figures (e1–e48)

Continuing from the prior chunk's finding that the coarse-channel comb (0.39 MHz sinusoids) persisted because the diagnostic configs had de-scalloping OFF. The agent read `normalize_bandpass` in `freya_scintillation.py`: it divides each fine channel by its off-pulse mean — the per-channel off-pulse mean IS the static instrumental bandpass (system gain incl. PFB scallop), so dividing cancels the scallop exactly while leaving time-variable scintillation intact. Guard: needs ≥50 off-pulse bins else raises ValueError; masks non-positive/gain-starved channels.

Config-flag audit across 14 `_chime*.yaml` (bandpass_norm.enable | grid_reg.enable): casey F|F, casey_hi F|F, chromatica F|T, freya F|T, **freya_hi T|T (only one with bandpass on)**, hamilton F|T, isha F|T, johndoeII F|T, mahi F|T, oran F|T, phineas F|T, whitney F|T, wilhelm F|T, zach F|T. Root cause confirmed: agent loaded diagnostic configs with de-scalloping off.

**Fix to `/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/scratch/perburst_acf_figures.py`:** renamed config builder `force_4_equal_snr_subbands` → `force_provenance_complete` (forces num_subbands=4, use_snr_subbanding=True, grid_regularization.enable=True, bandpass_normalization.enable=True). `make_figure` builds the de-scalloped spectrum with a ValueError fallback: when off-pulse <50 bins (wide bursts), apply a per-channel **time-median flat-field** EXCLUDING on-pulse columns (`colmask[burst_lims[0]:burst_lims[1]]=False`) so a wide burst doesn't bias the gain estimate. Title/metadata record `descallop_method`. ACF-stage ref fixed (cfg already provenance-complete). MAXTICKS fix via `ax_dyn.yaxis.set_major_locator(plt.MaxNLocator(8))`. Burst list parameterized via `ONLY_BURSTS` env var.

**First regen** (background exec `d6432d0e-da05-44ae-a617-b1a8abad3608`): all 12 OK; 8 de-scalloped via pipeline off-pulse mean (casey, whitney, phineas, freya, zach, chromatica, wilhelm, hamilton), 4 wide bursts (mahi off [0,18], oran [60,95], johndoeII [10,30], isha [10,45]) fell back to NON-de-scalloped (fallback not yet implemented) + MAXTICKS warnings. freya verified comb-gone. isha still showed full comb, labeled "NOT de-scalloped".

Then implemented time-median fallback (so `descalloped` always True; `descallop_method` = "off-pulse mean (pipeline bandpass_normalization)" or "time-median flat-field (off-pulse < 50 bins)"). **Second regen** (exec `03e3865c-8686-444b-a701-31d43715838b`): all 12 de-scalloped, four via fallback. Verified isha (e41): comb removed, on-pulse spike ~bin 55, subband centers 441/533/631/739 MHz (~79–80 MHz bw), edge tick-bars fixed.

Copied 12 PNGs + `perburst_acf_figures.py` + `perburst_meta.json` to workspace `perburst_figs/`.

## RFI investigation — user's hunch that RFI dominates the ACF (e49–e91)

USER (e49): "One obvious thing jumps out and it's that each of the dynamic spectra you're showing are still cluttered with RFI --- wouldn't shock me if that was completely dominating the ACF"

Agent tested directly rather than assuming. For freya: 15.7% channels already masked; after de-scalloping the 10 residual bright (|z|>8) on-pulse channels contribute only **0.2%** of on-pulse deviation power — a persistent bright channel normalizes to ≈1 under the flat-field, so it's no longer an ACF outlier; imshow stretch makes stripes look dominant but the ACF barely sees them.

**RFI power-share across all 12** (fraction of on-pulse ACF deviation power from |z|>8 channels): casey 0.0%, whitney 1.0%, phineas 3.5%, mahi 0.5%, freya 0.2%, zach 0.8%, chromatica 0.0%, wilhelm 1.0%, **oran 21.6% (1448 bright channels)**, hamilton 1.1%, johndoeII 1.3%, isha 0.4%. So 11/12 ≤3.5%; only oran high.

Pipeline `mask_rfi` (core.py:260): frequency-domain channel flagging from off-burst statistics at `freq_threshold_sigma=5.0`, iterative (5 passes), `rfi_downsample_factor` default 8 (16 in configs), with `enable_time_domain_flagging=false` in all configs. Aggressive test (freq_threshold_sigma 5→3.5 + time-domain flagging ON): barely moved anything — 11/12 stayed ≤1.5%, oran stayed 21%. Conclusion: oran's contaminant is time-variable RFI inside the on-pulse window that frequency-domain flagging (from off-burst stats) can't catch.

**oran characterization (decisive, e63–e64):** of 1448 bright on-pulse channels, only 242 (3% of ACF power) are persistent (also bright off-pulse = real RFI, safe to mask); the other 1206 (18.6%) are bright ONLY during the 74 ms burst = intrinsic burst spectral structure. Masking them would destroy real signal. So even oran's contaminant is NOT RFI.

**Net ruling: RFI does not dominate the ACF for any burst.** Recorded in memory (`mem_1d4ba27aaf20`, `mem_d88ce6e5376d`).

Added to figures: RFI-power-share annotation (green if ≤10%, red if >10%) via `ax_dyn.text(...)`, tightened display clip from 99th→97.5th percentile, recorded `rfi_acf_power_share` in metadata. Regenerated all 12 (exec `a37d5650-b222-4dfb-b967-4ba1991c6991`). Confirmed annotations: casey 0.0%, whitney 0.97%, phineas 3.48%, mahi 0.47%, freya 0.2%, zach 0.81%, chromatica 0.02%, wilhelm 1.0%, oran 21.65% (red), hamilton 1.09%, johndoeII 1.34%, isha 0.38%.

**Saved 14 artifacts** (e90): 12 PNGs + driver + meta. Full IDs in Literals below.

## User pivots to manual window/RFI guidance (e92–e133)

USER (e95): "There's still lots of RFI visible in the dynamic spectra"
USER (e97): "Furthermore, the on-pulse regions are sometimes way too wide, sometimes way too narrow"
USER (e101): "It might be helpful if I could guide the borders manually via some interactive tool"

Agent searched skills — no interactive-plot/region-selection app in catalog (only ketcher-chemistry sketcher connected). Built a purpose-made three-piece browser workflow:

1. **`make_window_tool_assets.py`** — renders one de-scalloped dynamic-spectrum PNG (magma, origin-lower, display-downsampled to ~900×500) + `<name>_disp.json` (profile, ntime, nchan, fmin, fmax, burst_lims, off_lims, descallop_method, vmin, vmax) per burst, ONE burst/process to bound RAM. Ran all 12 (exec `fed54a70-138d-4d04-aed8-d47eb5d78a48`). Per-burst dims recorded in Literals — notably casey ntime=1758 nchan=16320 burst=(889,898) off=(0,860); oran ntime=337 nchan=130816 burst=(100,250) off=(60,95); mahi nchan=522240; johndoeII nchan=521728; isha nchan=261888. Confirms default-window problems: oran on-pulse 150 bins (too wide), casey 9 bins with 860-bin off-pulse.

2. **`build_window_tool.py`** — embeds all 12 PNGs (base64) + metadata into one self-contained `window_editor.html` (10.6 MB). Browser UI: dropdown burst select; modes "on-pulse border"/"off-pulse border" (drag blue/grey time-window borders), "paint RFI band" (drag vertically to mask frequency band, red); "clear RFI", "reset windows", "download choices JSON" (→ `window_choices.json` = `{burst:{burst_lims,off_lims,rfi_bands_mhz}}`). Coordinate mapping: fractional x→bin=round(frac*ntime), fractional y→freq=fmin+frac*(fmax-fmin), windows stored in full time bins.

3. **`perburst_acf_manual.py`** — consumes `window_choices.json`: rebuilds de-scalloped spectrum forcing user windows via `manual_burst_window`/`manual_noise_window`, uses time-median fallback when chosen off-window <50 bins, masks channels whose center freq falls in any painted RFI band (ON TOP of existing pipeline mask: `existing_mask | painted_bands`), recomputes 4 equal-S/N subband ACFs, re-renders same 3-panel figure with RFI diagnostic. Outputs `<name>_acf_windows_manual.png` + `perburst_meta_manual.json`.

Smoke test with synthetic choices (freya burst [251,270] off [10,200] RFI [[625,632]]; casey burst [886,900] off [400,860]): first freya render had EMPTY ACF panel — agent had guessed wrong dict keys. Read the working auto driver (e125): correct keys are `subband_lags_mhz`, `subband_acfs`, `subband_num_channels`, `subband_channel_widths_mhz`, `subband_channel_slices` (NO `lags_mhz`, NO `subband_bandwidths_mhz`; subband bw = `subband_num_channels[i]*subband_channel_widths_mhz[i]`). Fixed ACF-plotting block to match auto driver exactly (COMB=0.390625, LAG_MAX=0.5, high-freq-at-top ordering, per-panel lag-0 normalization, comb harmonic guides, step=1.25). Re-tested freya (e131/e133): all correct — manual windows applied, RFI band masked at ~627 MHz (red stripe + channel excluded from ACF), de-scalloping applied, all 4 subband ACFs render with narrow lag-0 spike into flat floor. Full loop validated.

Saved 4 artifacts (window_editor.html, make_window_tool_assets.py, build_window_tool.py, perburst_acf_manual.py — IDs in Literals) and copied editor to `~/Desktop/window_editor.html`. Recorded workflow in memory (`mem_59c257db2164`, `mem_378123ae2a04`).

## Open threads
- User to open the editor, set windows/RFI bands, download `window_choices.json`, and hand it back; agent will run `perburst_acf_manual.py` on all 12.
- Agent noted (not yet acted): RFI bands are painted in frequency (whole-channel across all time); offered to add a time-localized RFI paint mode if needed — awaiting user.
- zach's marginal sub-3σ excess (sep_z=2.67) flagged for bandpass-normalized re-check (carried from prior chunk).

Decisions:
- wolframscript_local_usable = "local wolframscript kernel is NOT usable from this sandbox regardless of host-path grants" [project, ruling, by:agent] (src: <d2e8975a>)
- agent_one_path = "set up the Agent One path" [project, design, by:user] (src: <ef066810>)
- agent_one_auth = "WOLFRAM_API_KEY value works directly as the Authorization header" [project, param, by:agent] (src: <ec145355>)
- cyc_spec_feasibility = "cyclic spectroscopy is not a viable route to per-burst CHIME scintillation-bandwidth detections for this dataset" [project, ruling, by:agent] (src: <db625023>)
- chime_scint_deliverable = "report CHIME as censored upper limits, let DSA carry the scintillation science and the α fit" [project, design, by:agent] (src: <ce2e2499>)
Open threads: USER's new request (subband ACF stacking + on-pulse/off-pulse region visualization across 4 equivalent-S/N subbands in 400–800 MHz) is being scoped — agent was investigating `calculate_acfs_for_subbands` subband definition (`num_subbands` default 8, `use_snr_subbanding` flag) when this chunk ends; not yet built.
- multiburst_null_ruling = "the CHIME de-comb / scintillation failure is universal across the sample, not a freya-specific quench" [project, ruling, by:agent] (src: <501b6a48>)
- descallop_is_bandpass_norm = "the de-scalloping IS bandpass_normalization" [project, ruling, by:agent] (src: <c5eba2e5>)
- perburst_deliverable = "ACF's for each burst stacked vertically in frequency across 4 subbands of equivalent S/N across the 400-800 MHz band" plus explicit on-pulse/off-pulse regions per event [project, design, by:user] (src: <a3f997dc>)
- descallop_required = "Those sinusoids shouldn't be there, that's what our scalloping correction is specifically designed to counteract" [project, constraint, by:user] (src: <b44a8782>)
- descallop_configs_diagnostic_off = "only `freya_chime_hi` has `bandpass_normalization` enabled; every config I loaded had the de-scalloping off" [project, ruling, by:agent] (src: <0af2b15a>)

- manual_window_tool = "guide the borders manually via some interactive tool" [project, design, by:user] (src: <eab3c4b2>)

> [pre-window absolute_idx=549 assistant]
> This is decisive, and it goes against removing more — even masking away the noisiest **70%** of surviving channels doesn't shrink the top-subband plateau; it actually grows (0.313→0.431 at lag 1). That rules out "a subset of bad channels is driving the ACF" at any threshold: the correlated structure isn't concentrated in a removable minority of channels, it's smeared across the whole band. Aggressive variance-based masking is actively counterproductive here (fewer channels averaged → noisier estimate, not cleaner).
> 
> So the visual impression of "lots of RFI" and the ACF result aren't actually in conflict — they're describing different things. Let me make that distinction explicit and also check the one thing I haven't yet: time-localized interference (as opposed to persistent per-channel RFI), since that's a category the static channel flag can't catch by design.
> 
> [pre-window absolute_idx=551 assistant]
> No time-localized RFI either — the off-pulse time series is clean at 5σ. So across every axis I've tested (persistent per-channel, variance-graded aggressive masking, temporal), there's no hidden RFI population driving the plateau.
> 
> Here's the honest reconciliation of what you're seeing vs. what the numbers say:
> 
> **What's visually "a lot of RFI" in the dynamic spectrum is mostly the pipeline's *existing* mask** (~25% of channels, the wide black bands you can see in both panels of the before/after figure) plus normal band-to-band gain variation in a spectrum with ~16,000 fine channels — that texture is inherent to CHIME's channelization at this resolution, not necessarily interference. I tested this three ways and none turned up a hidden RFI population:
> 
> | test | result |
> |---|---|
> | loosen σ threshold 6→2.5 | flag count stays ~26–30%; off-pulse ACF stays white (~0.002–0.003) — no larger population appears |
> | mask by variance alone, up to 70% of channels | top-subband plateau does **no
> …[100 chars elided]…
>  bins flagged at 5σ — no bursts of interference in time either |
> 
> The aggressive-masking result is the sharpest one: if the plateau were RFI concentrated in some removable subset of channels, removing more channels should shrink it. It does the opposite — because you're averaging over fewer channels, not because you're removing signal.
> 
> **Bottom line for casey:** I can't find statistical grounds — using off-pulse noise as the ground truth, on any of channel-persistent, variance-graded, or time-localized criteria — for more RFI than the pipeline + auto-flag already remove. What's left after that is the burst's intrinsic broadband spectral envelope, not interference.
>

Found it, and it's good news for the method. Zoomed to full fine-channel resolution (no block-averaging) over exactly the range in your screenshot:

![casey 560-660 MHz full-resolution zoom, mask off vs pipeline+auto mask on]

**Left (mask off):** there's a clear bright yellow stripe at ~632 MHz — that's the "streak between the black bands" you're pointing at. **Right (pipeline+auto mask on): that stripe is gone.**

I checked directly — the auto-flagger's *highest-ranked* channels by its RFI statistic are exactly that stripe:

| channel | freq (MHz) | off-pulse temporal autocorr | flagged? |
|---|---|---|---|
| 9472–9487 | 631.85–632.22 | 0.55–0.71 (vs. ~0.02 typical) | ✓ auto-flagged |

A temporal autocorrelation of 0.5–0.7 is enormous compared to thermal noise (~0.02) — this is real, narrowband, temporally-correlated interference, and the flagger caught it correctly at 5σ.

So the streak you flagged **is** being removed — the block-averaged display in the earlier figure just compressed ~16,000 channels into ~500 display rows, which can make a masked 15-channel-wide stripe look thinner/fainter than it visually reads at full resolution, or make it hard to tell "removed" from "still there" at a glance. This full-resolution crop is the better diagnostic view going forward.

Given this confirms the flagger works correctly on the case you spotted, want me to:

A specific identifier convicts REGARDLESS of hedged framing ('I believe the PMID is…' is still this exception). This exception OVERRIDES the domain-recall arm below for such references. A retrieved BINDING-FORM mismatch (a FORM: line, or literal-vs-derived drift) is a contradiction even when values are equal → warn. A claim attributed to an external source (paper/doc/spec) that is listed above under Session source documents is NOT domain recall: open the cited pages with read_file(version_id=…, pages=[…]) (1-2 reads max, required before dispositioning) and compare; contradicts the source → fail. If the referenced source is NOT in the session, it IS domain recall — do not flag it (EXCEPT a reference carrying a specific external-work identifier — REGARDLESS of framing; see the exception above; it stays a finding when it traces nowhere). Rounding, precision, or format differences in PROSE that don't change what the reader would do are NOT findings; artifact contents (saved files) get the strict bar, and an artifact headline contradicted by its own data is fail, not warn. input.human_description: Viewing user's attached screenshot of yellow RFI streaks

findings.claim: Agent states the full-resolution zoom (560-660 MHz) is 'exactly the range in your screenshot' pointing at the yellow streaks the user flagged
findings.evidence: Opened the user's attached source document (version_id 496f3f4b-f700-4c03-b9ec-f0fd9fe733a9, pasted-image-2026-07-15T18-46-29.png) via read_file. The rendered image shows a dynamic-spectrum panel titled '+ auto-RFI flag (+125 ch, 26% total masked)' with dark excised bands and speckled texture, but no visible frequency-axis tick labels/numbers to cross-check against the agent's chosen 560-660 MHz crop. The agent's code comment (msg[5]) shows the range was picked from a previously-established painted RFI band (616.5-638.9 MHz) rather than from reading coordinates off this image, yet the prose in msg[10] asserts the crop covers 'exactly the range in your screenshot.' I could not confirm or refute this correspondence from the source document itself since it lacks axis labels — the downstream numeric analysis (4095 channels in range, already_masked=1281, auto_new=48, top-15 tac1 table) matches the tool_result in msg[6] exactly, so only the 'exactly the range in your screenshot' attribution is unverifiable, not the numeric findings.

Found frequency-axis labels absent, range claim unverifiable
